name: mock-server
description: Create and manage mock API servers for development and testing.
capabilities: Generate mock API endpoints from OpenAPI specifications
Create custom mock responses with simulated delays and errors
Mock REST and GraphQL APIs using multiple tools
Implement authentication simulation and error conditions
Configure rate limiting and conditional responses
Set up proxy to real APIs with record and replay
author: CuriousLearner
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only a SKILL.md file with guidance on creating mock API servers. The static analyzer incorrectly flagged 95 documentation code examples as security concerns. All findings are false positives - the skill has no executable code, network calls, file system access, or environment variable handling. The analyzer mistook documentation examples (curl commands, Express code, MSW handlers) for executable code.
valueStatement: Developers need realistic API simulations during development and testing. This skill provides guidance on creating mock servers with json-server, MSW, Prism, and custom Express implementations.
userTitle: Create Mock API Servers for Testing
limitations: Does not install or run mock servers automatically
Does not access external APIs or network resources
Does not modify local files or system configuration
Provides guidance only - user implements actual servers

useCases.title: Simulate APIs During UI Development
useCases.description: Create mock endpoints to build user interfaces before backend services are ready

useCases.title: Test Error Scenarios
useCases.description: Simulate network failures, timeouts, and various HTTP error codes for comprehensive testing

useCases.title: Prototype API Contracts
useCases.description: Design and validate API specifications before implementation begins

promptTemplates.title: Basic Mock Server
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up a json-server with mock data for users and posts endpoints on port 3000

promptTemplates.title: OpenAPI Mock
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a mock server from this OpenAPI specification using Prism with dynamic responses

promptTemplates.title: Error Simulation
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure mock endpoints that return 500 errors randomly and simulate 30-second timeouts

promptTemplates.title: GraphQL Mock
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up an Apollo Server mock with resolvers for users and posts queries and createUser mutation

bestPractices: Mirror production API structure and response formats for realistic testing
Use realistic data generators like Faker.js for comprehensive test coverage
Implement proper error responses including 400, 401, 403, 404, 409, and 500 status codes
antiPatterns: Using static mock data that does not reflect real API behavior
Ignoring error scenario testing and only testing happy paths
Failing to reset mock server state between test runs

faq.question: Which mock server tool should I choose?
faq.answer: JSON Server for quick prototypes, MSW for browser testing, Prism for OpenAPI specs, Express for full control, WireMock for enterprise needs.

faq.question: Can I mock GraphQL APIs?
faq.answer: Yes, use Apollo Server with mocks or MockList for dynamic data generation. Supports queries, mutations, and complex resolvers.

faq.question: How do I simulate network errors?
faq.answer: Use MSW http.get() with HttpResponse.error() or setTimeout for timeouts. Random error injection with Math.random() checks.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using mock servers?
faq.answer: Mock servers run locally. No data leaves your machine. Use in-memory storage or temporary files that are not persisted.

faq.question: Can I integrate with CI/CD pipelines?
faq.answer: Yes, run mock servers as background processes in tests. Use MSW for Node.js integration tests or start Express server before test suite.

faq.question: How is this different from Postman mock servers?
faq.answer: This skill helps you create local mock servers you control. Postman mocks are cloud-hosted. Local mocks are faster and free.
